messages.content:
THE TEST: Name the artifact that would SETTLE this claim — not inform it,
settle it. If a purchasable report, an existing dataset, or a week of expert
calls would settle it, the claim is an ERROR: our model of the world is wrong
today and research fixes it. If only time settles it (a market shift that
hasn't happened, a rival's response, a demand gate that may or may not bind),
it is a RISK.

Guidance:
- "The number is X, not Y" claims about TODAY's market are errors
  (market size, shares, definitions, scope mismatches, benchmark misuse).
- Claims about how the next 12–24 months UNFOLD are risks
  (substitution pace, foreclosure, construction slips, price responses).
- Mixed claims: classify by the load-bearing part. If correcting today's
  number removes most of the expected loss, it is an error.
- For an ERROR, propose the concrete correction the evidence implies
  (nodeId, corrected value, band, one-sentence rationale) and 2 targeted
  research queries (one confirming, one falsifying) with concrete entities.
- Do NOT propose corrections for risks; their perturbation already expresses
  the scenario.

content: {"resolution":"error","settleTest":"A Europe- or CE-7-scoped capacity split (from CBRE, Structure Research, or Synergy regional data) showing hyperscale-owned vs colocation share of Central European data-center capacity today. This is a purchasable/existing dataset, not a future event.","proposedCorrection":{"nodeId":"seg.hyperscale","value":0.28,"low":0.2,"high":0.4,"rationale":"CBRE/Structure Research show CE hyperscale self-build is concentrated outside CE-7 (60% in IE/NL/SE/BE) and hyperscalers in Frankfurt mostly rent colo, so the CE hyperscale-owned share is well below the 0.44 global figure and colocation carries more weight."},"researchQueries":[{"query":"Structure Research or CBRE CE-7 / Frankfurt data center capacity split by owner type: hyperscale self-build vs colocation share of operational MW in Poland, Czechia, Austria, Hungary","kind":"confirming"},{"query":"Synergy Research or CBRE Europe-scoped capacity split showing hyperscale-owned capacity at 40% or more of Central European (CE-7) total data-center capacity","kind":"falsifying"}]}
